reasoning: The informal statement says: if G has exactly one subgroup H of order n, then H is normal. The Lean formalization fixes H as a subgroup of G and encodes "H is the unique subgroup of its order" as `∀ K, Nat.card K = Nat.card H → K = H`. This is exactly the standard encoding of uniqueness: given that H has some order n = Nat.card H, saying "H is the only subgroup of order n" is logically equivalent to saying every subgroup K with the same cardinality as H equals H. The conclusion `H.Normal` matches "H is a normal subgroup of G". The quantifier structure, types (arbitrary group G, arbitrary subgroup H, Nat.card for order), and the implication direction all faithfully correspond to the informal statement.
